title: Dimensionality reduction

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Dimensionality reduction; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: scikit-learn User Guide; 1.9.x target, accessed 2026-07; User Guide sections 2, 3.4.7, and 7.5: clustering, novelty/outlier detection, decomposition, and evaluation; atomic topic: Dimensionality reduction
sources.claim: Dimensionality reduction maps data into fewer coordinates to preserve a stated property such as linear variance, neighborhood, or reconstruction, inevitably losing other information.

sources.locator: CS2023 Artificial Intelligence; August 2023; Machine Learning: unsupervised methods, clustering, dimensionality, and recommendation concepts; atomic topic: Dimensionality reduction
sources.claim: Dimensionality reduction maps data into fewer coordinates to preserve a stated property such as linear variance, neighborhood, or reconstruction, inevitably losing other information.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Dimensionality reduction maps data into fewer coordinates to preserve a stated property such as linear variance, neighborhood, or reconstruction, inevitably losing other information.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: PCA is fitted on standardized training features, explained variance and reconstruction loss are reported, and downstream evaluation uses the same fitted transform. Your goal is to apply a linear projection and explain information retained and lost. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Data and experimental workflow; Bias, variance and regularization; Features and preprocessing. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: Dimensionality reduction maps data into fewer coordinates to preserve a stated property such as linear variance, neighborhood, or reconstruction, inevitably losing other information. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: Components may be hard to interpret, scaling matters, nonlinear visualizations distort distances, separated points need not be classes, fit must avoid leakage, and projection cannot recover omitted context. The learner must reject this overclaim: A two-dimensional projection preserves all important structure, and visually separated groups prove the original data contains discrete classes.

includes: Use low-dimensional projections for compression or exploration without overinterpreting visual separation.
Apply a linear projection and explain information retained and lost.
excludes: Components may be hard to interpret, scaling matters, nonlinear visualizations distort distances, separated points need not be classes, fit must avoid leakage, and projection cannot recover omitted context.

outcomes: Apply a linear projection and explain information retained and lost.

examples.title: Dimensionality reduction: bounded direct case
examples.situation: PCA is fitted on standardized training features, explained variance and reconstruction loss are reported, and downstream evaluation uses the same fitted transform.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: PCA is fitted on standardized training features, explained variance and reconstruction loss are reported, and downstream evaluation uses the same fitted transform.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Name the property the projection preserves, quantify retained and lost information, fit it within the evaluation split, and avoid turning visual separation into a class claim.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Name the property the projection preserves, quantify retained and lost information, fit it within the evaluation split, and avoid turning visual separation into a class claim.

examples.title: Dimensionality reduction: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: A two-dimensional visualization shows apparent islands that move with parameters, so it is used for exploration rather than evidence of distinct populations.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: A two-dimensional visualization shows apparent islands that move with parameters, so it is used for exploration rather than evidence of distinct populations.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: Components may be hard to interpret, scaling matters, nonlinear visualizations distort distances, separated points need not be classes, fit must avoid leakage, and projection cannot recover omitted context.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: A two-dimensional projection preserves all important structure, and visually separated groups prove the original data contains discrete classes.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Apply a linear projection and explain information retained and lost.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A two-dimensional projection preserves all important structure, and visually separated groups prove the original data contains discrete classes.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: A two-dimensional projection preserves all important structure, and visually separated groups prove the original data contains discrete classes.

analogy.example: an exploratory sorting table whose representation, distance, projection, reference population, and exposure policy determine visible patterns can introduce Dimensionality reduction only after its canonical technical definition and evidence boundary are stated.
usefulFor: clustering, dimensionality reduction, anomaly scoring, and recommendation
limitations: algorithmic groups and outliers are parameter-dependent summaries rather than discovered human categories or harms
